nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root React application component that sets up client-side routing, holds top-level user/cart state shared via context, and shows a location-permission prompt on load.

nodes.name: LocationPrompt
nodes.summary: Modal prompting the user to share their location, handling geolocation permission requests and timeout/error states.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Top-level app shell wiring up React Router routes, cart/user state, and the header/footer/cart drawer layout.

nodes.name: client.ts
nodes.summary: Central typed fetch wrapper exposing every backend API call used by the client, covering cart operations, catalog search, subscriptions, predictions, checkout and auth.
nodes.languageNotes: A single shared `request()` helper wraps fetch/JSON handling so each exported function stays a thin, typed one-liner.

nodes.name: request
nodes.summary: Shared low-level fetch helper that issues an HTTP request against the API base URL and parses the JSON response.

nodes.name: postOutcome
nodes.summary: Submits free-text shopping intent to build a cart outcome.

nodes.name: postVoiceIntent
nodes.summary: Submits a voice transcript to the intent API to build or update a cart.

nodes.name: postVisionAnalyze
nodes.summary: Uploads an image (multipart form) plus optional text to the vision analysis endpoint to build a cart.

nodes.name: postShareParse
nodes.summary: Submits a shared link or text to the share-parse endpoint to extract a cart.

nodes.name: postConstraint
nodes.summary: Submits budget, servings and optional text constraints to build a budget-aware cart.

nodes.name: postCartOp
nodes.summary: Applies a single cart operation (add/remove/update quantity) for a given session.

nodes.name: getCart
nodes.summary: Fetches the current cart state for a session.

nodes.name: searchCatalog
nodes.summary: Searches the product catalog by query, category and result limit.

nodes.name: getProduct
nodes.summary: Fetches a single product by id.

nodes.name: postStockOverride
nodes.summary: Admin call overriding a product's in-stock status.

nodes.name: searchRecommend
nodes.summary: Fetches recommended products for a search query.

nodes.name: getSubscribedCart
nodes.summary: Fetches a cart built from a user's active subscriptions.

nodes.name: getPredictionInsights
nodes.summary: Fetches personalized reorder prediction insights for a user.

nodes.name: addSubscription
nodes.summary: Creates a new recurring product subscription for a user.

nodes.name: removeSubscription
nodes.summary: Cancels a user's subscription to a product.

nodes.name: getUserSubscriptions
nodes.summary: Fetches all active subscriptions for a user.

nodes.name: getDueSubscriptions
nodes.summary: Fetches subscriptions currently due for reorder.

nodes.name: getAllSubscriptionsCart
nodes.summary: Fetches a combined cart of all of a user's subscribed items.

nodes.name: getUserPreferences
nodes.summary: Fetches stored shopping preferences for a user.

nodes.name: getUserPantry
nodes.summary: Fetches the inferred pantry/stock state for a user.

nodes.name: postReplan
nodes.summary: Submits feedback and cart context to replan/adjust an existing cart.

nodes.name: postOutcomePersonalized
nodes.summary: Builds a personalized cart outcome for a specific user from free-text intent.

nodes.name: getCounterfactuals
nodes.summary: Fetches counterfactual/alternative product candidates for a given cart need.

nodes.name: placeOrder
nodes.summary: Places an order for a session, user and payment method.

nodes.name: getOrderHistory
nodes.summary: Fetches a user's past order history.

nodes.name: registerUser
nodes.summary: Registers a new user account with profile details.

nodes.name: loginUser
nodes.summary: Authenticates a user with email and password.

nodes.name: CartDrawer.tsx
nodes.summary: Slide-out cart panel with recommended/economical tabs, per-item quantity controls, budget summary and a checkout call-to-action.

nodes.name: CartDrawer
nodes.summary: Main cart drawer component rendering recommended/economical tabs, item rows with quantity controls, budget summary and checkout button.

nodes.name: Composer.tsx
nodes.summary: Primary multi-mode input composer (text, voice, budget) used on the home page to capture shopping intent and trigger cart building.

nodes.name: looksLikeSingleItemSearch
nodes.summary: Heuristic that decides whether typed text looks like a single-item search query rather than a full meal/intent request.

nodes.name: Composer
nodes.summary: Multi-mode composer component switching between text, voice and budget input tabs to submit shopping intent.

nodes.name: Footer.tsx
nodes.summary: Site footer with newsletter signup, PWA install prompt trigger, and category/legal navigation links.

nodes.name: Footer
nodes.summary: Footer component rendering newsletter signup, PWA install button (with iOS hint), and navigation link groups.

nodes.name: NowCartVerified.tsx
nodes.summary: Small reusable badge component that renders a "NowCart Verified" trust signal on product listings.

nodes.name: NowCartVerified
nodes.summary: Renders a small "NowCart Verified" badge with configurable size.

nodes.name: ProductCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable product tile showing image, rating stars, pricing and add/remove-from-cart controls, used across shop, search and home pages.

nodes.name: ProductCard
nodes.summary: Product tile showing rating stars, price, discount and add/remove-from-cart controls.

nodes.name: EngineTrail.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders a human-readable "why this happened" trail translating the cart-building engine's internal reasoning steps into plain language.

nodes.name: humanStep
nodes.summary: Translates a raw engine reasoning-trail string into a human-readable label and icon by matching known step patterns.

nodes.name: EngineTrail
nodes.summary: Renders the deduplicated, humanized list of engine reasoning steps in an expandable trail UI.

nodes.name: HitlPrompt.tsx
nodes.summary: Small human-in-the-loop confirmation prompt asking the user to proceed with the current cart or view alternatives.

nodes.name: HitlPrompt
nodes.summary: Renders a question with "proceed" and "show alternatives" action buttons for human-in-the-loop confirmation.

nodes.name: ReplanBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Inline chat bar letting users give free-text or quick-chip feedback to swap items or replan the current cart.

nodes.name: resolveUserId
nodes.summary: Derives a stable user id string from a stored user object, falling back to deriving one from the email.

nodes.name: ReplanBar
nodes.summary: Chat-style bar for cart replanning, supporting quick-chip direct swaps and free-text feedback submitted to the replan API.

nodes.name: WhyThisOne.tsx
nodes.summary: Expandable panel that humanizes the engine's reasoning trail and confidence score for why a specific product was chosen.

nodes.name: humaniseReason
nodes.summary: Converts a raw selection-reason string and confidence score into a human-friendly explanation sentence.

nodes.name: WhyThisOne
nodes.summary: Expandable panel showing the humanized reasoning and trail for why a cart item was selected.

nodes.name: FrontDoorPanel.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal dispatcher that renders the correct "front door" intent-capture panel (constrain/predict/share/show/speak) based on the selected door.

nodes.name: FrontDoorPanel
nodes.summary: Dispatches to the correct panel component (Constrain/Predict/Share/Show/Speak) based on the selected front door id.

nodes.name: PanelResult.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared result-summary UI (cart preview, item list, totals) rendered after any front-door panel finishes building a cart.

nodes.name: PanelResult
nodes.summary: Displays a summary of the built cart (items, totals, remaining budget, shortfall) with a caption and view-cart action.

nodes.name: doors.tsx
nodes.summary: Static configuration array defining the five front-door entry points (constrain, predict, share, show, speak) with their icons and labels.

nodes.name: ConstrainPanel.tsx
nodes.summary: Front-door panel for capturing a budget and serving size to build a budget-constrained cart via the constraint API.

nodes.name: ConstrainPanel
nodes.summary: Two-phase panel collecting budget and servings, submitting them to the constraint API, then showing the resulting cart.

nodes.name: PredictPanel.tsx
nodes.summary: Front-door panel for subscription-based predictive reordering: manages recurring subscriptions, due-for-reorder carts, pantry tracking and personalized prediction insights.

nodes.name: resolveUserId
nodes.summary: Derives a stable user id from the stored user object for subscription/prediction API calls.

nodes.name: BrandPicker
nodes.summary: Debounced product/brand search picker used to select a product when creating a new subscription.

nodes.name: PredictPanel
nodes.summary: Main predictive-reorder panel managing subscriptions, due-cart, prediction insights and pantry state, and building a subscribed cart.

nodes.name: SharePanel.tsx
nodes.summary: Front-door panel that accepts a shared recipe/social link or pasted text and parses it into a cart via the share-parse API.

nodes.name: SharePanel
nodes.summary: Panel accepting a shared link/text, submitting it to the share-parse API, then showing the resulting cart.

nodes.name: ShowPanel.tsx
nodes.summary: Front-door panel for image-based intent capture, letting users take a photo or upload an image analyzed by the vision API to build a cart.

nodes.name: ShowPanel
nodes.summary: Panel for capturing an image via camera or gallery upload, submitting it (with optional text) to the vision-analyze API.

nodes.name: SpeakPanel.tsx
nodes.summary: Front-door panel for voice-based intent capture using the Web Speech API, including live transcription, follow-up voice commands, and cart updates.

nodes.name: getRecognition
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and configures a browser SpeechRecognition instance if available.

nodes.name: SpeakPanel
nodes.summary: Voice intent-capture panel managing microphone permission, live transcription via Web Speech API, and follow-up voice commands against the cart.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Application bootstrap entry point that mounts the React root and imports global CSS.

nodes.name: AboutPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Static "About Us" marketing page listing the team and company mission.

nodes.name: AboutPage
nodes.summary: Renders the static About page with team member cards and mission copy.

nodes.name: AdminDashboardPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Internal admin dashboard showing usage statistics, scaling info, and LLM/AWS cost breakdowns with periodic auto-refresh.

nodes.name: KpiCard
nodes.summary: Small stat-tile component showing an icon, title, value and sub-label for the admin dashboard KPIs.

nodes.name: MiniBar
nodes.summary: Small horizontal bar-chart component rendering a labeled value against a max scale.

nodes.name: AdminDashboardPage
nodes.summary: Admin dashboard page fetching and periodically refreshing usage stats, scaling info and LLM/AWS cost data, rendered across tabbed sections.

nodes.name: DeliveryInfoPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Static page describing delivery zones, timing windows and delivery policies.

nodes.name: DeliveryInfoPage
nodes.summary: Renders the static delivery info page describing zones, timing and policies.

nodes.name: HomePage.tsx
nodes.summary: Landing page presenting the front-door intent panels and featured/recommended products to start a shopping session.

nodes.name: HomePage
nodes.summary: Landing page rendering the front-door intent panels grid and a featured products section.

nodes.name: LoginPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Login and registration page handling user authentication against the backend auth API.

nodes.name: LoginPage
nodes.summary: Combined login/registration form page that authenticates against the auth API and stores the session user.

nodes.name: OrderHistoryPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Page listing a signed-in user's past orders fetched from the backend order-history API.

nodes.name: resolveUserId
nodes.summary: Derives a stable user id from the stored user object for order-history lookups.

nodes.name: OrderHistoryPage
nodes.summary: Fetches and renders a signed-in user's past orders.

nodes.name: OrderSuccessPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Post-checkout confirmation page summarizing the placed order, payment method and estimated delivery time.

nodes.name: OrderSuccessPage
nodes.summary: Post-checkout confirmation page summarizing the order, payment method and estimated delivery window.

nodes.name: PaymentPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Checkout page handling delivery address, payment method selection (UPI, cards, wallets) and order placement, with numerous small icon/row sub-components.

nodes.name: resolveUserId
nodes.summary: Derives a stable user id from the stored user object for placing the order.

nodes.name: methodLabel
nodes.summary: Maps an internal payment method code to its human-readable display label.

nodes.name: Row
nodes.summary: Small labeled key/value row component used in the order summary section.

nodes.name: QrIcon
nodes.summary: Renders the QR-code payment method icon (inline SVG).

nodes.name: PaymentPage
nodes.summary: Checkout page managing delivery address, payment method selection and order placement against the backend.

nodes.name: PrivacyPolicyPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Static privacy policy legal page.

nodes.name: PrivacyPolicyPage
nodes.summary: Renders the static privacy policy legal page.

nodes.name: ProductPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Single product detail page with pricing, description and add/remove-from-cart actions.

nodes.name: ProductPage
nodes.summary: Product detail page showing full product info with add/remove-from-cart controls.

nodes.name: SearchResultsPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Search results page rendering products matching a catalog search query.

nodes.name: ProductResultCard
nodes.summary: Search-result variant of the product card, showing match relevance alongside standard product details.

nodes.name: SearchResultsPage
nodes.summary: Page rendering catalog search results for a query, including empty and loading states.

nodes.name: ShopPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Category browsing page listing products with filtering by category and search term.

nodes.name: ShopPage
nodes.summary: Category browsing page listing and filtering products by category and search term.

nodes.name: SubscriptionsPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Page for managing recurring product subscriptions, including adding new subscriptions via brand search and adjusting frequency.

nodes.name: resolveUserId
nodes.summary: Derives a stable user id from the stored user object for subscription management calls.

nodes.name: BrandPicker
nodes.summary: Debounced product/brand search picker used to add a new subscription from this page.

nodes.name: SubscriptionsPage
nodes.summary: Page for viewing and managing all of a user's recurring subscriptions, including adding, removing and adjusting frequency.

nodes.name: TermsPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Static terms & conditions legal page.

nodes.name: TermsPage
nodes.summary: Renders the static terms & conditions legal page.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting shared UI primitives (Button, Card, Chip, Panel, Spinner, EmptyState, ErrorState, Toast provider, animation helpers) used across the client.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the agents module that re-exports the LangGraph pipeline builder, node functions, and shared agent state for use by the outcome service.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Builds the LangGraph StateGraph wiring together intent, decompose, match, confidence, counterfactual, and replan nodes with conditional routing edges for the Outcome Engine pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses LangGraph's add_conditional_edges with small routing helper functions (_should_replan, _replan_route) to decide whether the graph should loop back into replanning.

nodes.name: nodes.py
nodes.summary: Implements the core LangGraph node functions (intent, decompose, match, confidence, counterfactual, replan) that make up the Decompose -> Match -> Optimize -> Confidence outcome pipeline, including LLM prompt construction and catalog matching logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Very large file (959 lines) combining prompt construction, async catalog search fan-out via asyncio.gather, and quantity normalization heuristics.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: Defines the AgentState schema shared across all LangGraph nodes, carrying raw input, needs, candidates, cart, confidence, and conversation context through the pipeline.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Aggregates and re-exports all FastAPI controller routers (admin, cart, catalog, auth, orders, outcome, share, vision, voice, etc.) for registration with the main app.

nodes.name: admin.py
nodes.summary: Admin-only FastAPI endpoints for overriding product stock status and triggering badge recomputation.

nodes.name: cart.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI endpoints for cart mutation operations (add/remove/update/clear items) and cart retrieval, delegating to CartOpsService.

nodes.name: constraint.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI endpoint that builds a budget-constrained cart by delegating to BudgetService.

nodes.name: health.py
nodes.summary: Simple health-check endpoint returning service status for uptime monitoring.

nodes.name: outcome.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI endpoint that accepts free-text intent requests and routes them through OutcomeService to produce a cart.

nodes.name: share.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI endpoint for parsing shared content (URLs or text) into a cart, including a demo-mode cart builder used when DEMO_ENABLED is set.

nodes.name: vision.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI endpoint that analyzes an uploaded image (or runs in demo mode) to extract a shopping cart via VisionService.

nodes.name: _normalize_quantity_to_packs
nodes.summary: Normalizes a free-form quantity and unit string into a pack count, handling units like kg, g, l, ml, and pieces.

nodes.name: intent_node
nodes.summary: First LangGraph node that parses raw user input to detect intent mode (e.g., dinner-party, restock) and serving size before decomposition.

nodes.name: decompose_node
nodes.summary: Core decomposition node that uses an LLM to break a raw intent into structured shopping needs (items, quantities, constraints), producing the Need list consumed by matching.

nodes.name: match_node
nodes.summary: Matches decomposed needs against the product catalog using hybrid semantic search and fuzzy matching, building cart items and economical alternatives concurrently via asyncio.gather.

nodes.name: confidence_node
nodes.summary: Computes an overall confidence score for the assembled cart and flags low-confidence items needing clarification.

nodes.name: counterfactual_node
nodes.summary: Generates "what-if" counterfactual alternatives for cart items using the counterfactual service, used to explain substitution reasoning.

nodes.name: replan_node
nodes.summary: Reprocesses a cart based on user feedback text, extracting new constraints (dietary, swaps, exclusions) and re-running matching to produce an updated cart.

nodes.name: AgentState
nodes.summary: State object threaded through every LangGraph node, holding raw input, needs, candidates, cart, confidence, user context, and replanning fields.

nodes.name: stock_override
nodes.summary: Admin endpoint that overrides a product's stock status in both the cache and the repository.

nodes.name: recompute_badges
nodes.summary: Admin endpoint that triggers recomputation of product badges via BadgeService.

nodes.name: cart_operation
nodes.summary: Dispatches cart mutation operations (add, remove, update, clear, total) to CartOpsService based on the requested op string.

nodes.name: get_cart
nodes.summary: Retrieves the current cart for a session via CartOpsService.

nodes.name: constraint_cart
nodes.summary: Endpoint that builds a budget-constrained cart using BudgetService and returns it as a CartResponse.

nodes.name: create_outcome
nodes.summary: Endpoint that submits free-text intent to OutcomeService and returns the resulting cart.

nodes.name: _build_demo_share_cart
nodes.summary: Builds a hardcoded demo cart used when share parsing runs in demo mode, bypassing the real LLM pipeline.

nodes.name: parse_share
nodes.summary: Endpoint that parses shared URL or text content into a cart, either via the demo cart builder or ShareService.parse_shared_content.

nodes.name: _build_demo_vision_cart
nodes.summary: Builds a hardcoded demo cart used when image analysis runs in demo mode.

nodes.name: analyze_image
nodes.summary: Endpoint that accepts an uploaded image (or demo mode) and returns a cart derived via VisionService.analyze_image.

nodes.name: voice.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI endpoint that handles voice transcript intents (or runs in demo mode) and routes them through OutcomeService to produce a cart.

nodes.name: cart.py
nodes.summary: Domain models for Cart, CartItem, and Substitution representing the core shopping cart entity manipulated throughout the outcome pipeline.

nodes.name: enums.py
nodes.summary: Defines core domain enumerations IntentMode and NeedStatus used to classify user intent and need resolution state.

nodes.name: need.py
nodes.summary: Domain model representing a single decomposed shopping need (name, quantity, unit, matched product) produced by the decompose node.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init that re-exports request and response DTO classes for use by controllers.

nodes.name: requests.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic request DTOs (OutcomeRequest, VoiceIntentRequest, ConstraintRequest, ShareRequest, CartOpRequest, SosRequest, StockOverrideRequest) defining the API's inbound payload contracts.

nodes.name: responses.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic response DTOs (HealthResponse, ProductResponse, CartResponse, etc.) with from_domain factory methods converting domain models into API-facing shapes.

nodes.name: budget_service.py
nodes.summary: Service that builds and trims a shopping cart to fit within a user-specified budget by iteratively dropping lowest-priority items.

nodes.name: outcome_service.py
nodes.summary: Central orchestration service that injects user context (profile, preferences, recent orders) and invokes the LangGraph outcome graph to turn intent into a cart; also supports feedback-driven replanning.

nodes.name: share_service.py
nodes.summary: Service that fetches and parses shared URLs or text (stripping HTML) and uses an LLM to extract shopping intent, then delegates to OutcomeService to build a cart.

nodes.name: vision_service.py
nodes.summary: Service that sends an uploaded image to a vision LLM provider to extract grocery items and quantities, then builds a cart via OutcomeService.

nodes.name: _build_demo_cart
nodes.summary: Builds a hardcoded demo cart used when voice intent processing runs in demo mode.

nodes.name: voice_intent
nodes.summary: Endpoint that processes a voice transcript (or demo query) and returns a cart via OutcomeService.

nodes.name: Substitution
nodes.summary: Domain model recording a single product substitution with original and replacement product details and the reason for the swap.

nodes.name: CartItem
nodes.summary: Domain model for a single cart line item including price, quantity, confidence, and substitution/out-of-stock metadata, with a computed line_total.

nodes.name: Cart
nodes.summary: Domain model for the full shopping cart including items, economical alternatives, totals, budget/shortfall tracking, and reasoning trail, with a recompute_total method.

nodes.name: IntentMode
nodes.summary: Enumeration of supported intent modes (e.g., quick-restock, dinner-party) used to steer decomposition and matching behavior.

nodes.name: NeedStatus
nodes.summary: Enumeration of resolution states for a decomposed need (e.g., matched, unmatched, clarification-needed).

nodes.name: Need
nodes.summary: Domain model representing a single decomposed shopping need with name, quantity, unit, category hint, and matched product id.

nodes.name: OutcomeRequest
nodes.summary: Request payload for submitting free-text or structured intent to the outcome engine.

nodes.name: VoiceIntentRequest
nodes.summary: Request payload carrying a voice transcript and session id.

nodes.name: ConstraintRequest
nodes.summary: Request payload for budget-constrained cart building (budget, servings, text).

nodes.name: ShareRequest
nodes.summary: Request payload for parsing shared URL or text content into a cart.

nodes.name: CartOpRequest
nodes.summary: Request payload for cart mutation operations (op, entity, quantity).

nodes.name: SosRequest
nodes.summary: Request payload for emergency/SOS situation requests.

nodes.name: StockOverrideRequest
nodes.summary: Request payload for admin stock override operations on a product.

nodes.name: ProductResponse
nodes.summary: Response DTO exposing catalog product fields with a from_domain factory converting the Product domain model.

nodes.name: SubstitutionResponse
nodes.summary: Response DTO for a product substitution with a from_domain factory.

nodes.name: CartItemResponse
nodes.summary: Response DTO for a single cart line item with a from_domain factory.

nodes.name: CartResponse
nodes.summary: Top-level response DTO for a full cart, assembling items, substitutions, and totals via from_domain.

nodes.name: BudgetService
nodes.summary: Encapsulates budget-constrained cart building, iteratively trimming lowest-priority items until the cart fits the budget.

nodes.name: get_budget_service
nodes.summary: Factory/singleton accessor returning a shared BudgetService instance.

nodes.name: OutcomeService
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full outcome pipeline: injects user context, invokes the LangGraph outcome graph, handles errors, caches the resulting cart, and supports feedback-driven replanning.

nodes.name: get_outcome_service
nodes.summary: Factory/singleton accessor returning a shared OutcomeService instance.

nodes.name: _extract_text_from_html
nodes.summary: Strips HTML tags/scripts/styles and decodes common entities to produce plain text for LLM parsing.

nodes.name: _fetch_url_content
nodes.summary: Fetches a shared URL over HTTP and extracts readable text content for downstream LLM parsing.

nodes.name: ShareService
nodes.summary: Parses shared URLs or text using an LLM to extract shopping intent, then delegates to OutcomeService to build a cart.

nodes.name: get_share_service
nodes.summary: Factory/singleton accessor returning a shared ShareService instance.

nodes.name: VisionService
nodes.summary: Sends an uploaded image to a vision LLM provider to extract grocery items and quantities, then builds a cart via OutcomeService.

nodes.name: get_vision_service
nodes.summary: Factory/singleton accessor returning a shared VisionService instance.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI controller exposing registration, login, and profile-lookup endpoints; hashes passwords, assigns roles by email heuristic, and reads/writes users via the repository layer.

nodes.name: RegisterRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic request schema for the registration endpoint capturing name, email, password, preferences, and location fields.

nodes.name: LoginRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic request schema holding email and password for the login endpoint.

nodes.name: AuthResponse
nodes.summary: Pydantic response schema returned by register/login containing user id, name, role, preferences, and demographic fields.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Registers a new user, generating a user id from the email, hashing the password, storing the user via the repository, and returning an AuthResponse.

nodes.name: login
nodes.summary: Authenticates a user by email/password against stored records and returns an AuthResponse on success, raising HTTPException on failure.

nodes.name: get_user_profile
nodes.summary: Fetches and returns a user's profile data by user id, raising a 404 if not found.

nodes.name: catalog.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI controller exposing product search, recommendation, and single-product lookup endpoints backed by the catalog service and repository.

nodes.name: search_products
nodes.summary: Search endpoint that delegates to CatalogService.search_products with query, category, and limit filters.

nodes.name: recommend_products
nodes.summary: Recommendation endpoint that returns a best-match product plus alternatives via CatalogService.recommend_products.

nodes.name: get_product
nodes.summary: Retrieves a single product by id from the repository, raising 404 if not found.

nodes.name: orders.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI controller handling order placement, item rating submission, and order-history retrieval, coordinating cart cache, repository, and badge service.

nodes.name: PlaceOrderRequest
nodes.summary: Request schema for placing an order, carrying session id, user id, and payment method.

nodes.name: OrderResponse
nodes.summary: Response schema describing a placed order including items, total, status, and payment status.

nodes.name: ItemRating
nodes.summary: Schema pairing a product id with a numeric rating value used in order-rating requests.

nodes.name: RateOrderRequest
nodes.summary: Request schema wrapping a list of ItemRating entries for the order-rating endpoint.

nodes.name: place_order
nodes.summary: Places an order from the user's active cart, persists it via the repository, clears the cached cart, and returns an OrderResponse.

nodes.name: rate_order_items
nodes.summary: Records per-item ratings for a placed order via the badge service and returns the recorded results.

nodes.name: get_order_history
nodes.summary: Returns a user's historical orders sorted by date via the repository.

nodes.name: subscribe.py
nodes.summary: Large FastAPI controller for the predictive-restock feature set: subscription management, predicted/due carts, preference insights, recently-ordered items, cart replanning, and counterfactual explanations.

nodes.name: ReplanRequest
nodes.summary: Request schema for replanning a cart based on free-text feedback, servings, and current cart items.

nodes.name: CounterfactualRequest
nodes.summary: Request schema identifying a cart need, the selected product, and rejected candidates for counterfactual explanation.

nodes.name: SubscriptionRequest
nodes.summary: Request schema for creating a recurring product subscription with a frequency.

nodes.name: get_prediction_insights
nodes.summary: Returns restock-prediction insight statistics for a user via the subscribe service.

nodes.name: add_subscription
nodes.summary: Creates a recurring subscription for a product via the subscribe service.

nodes.name: remove_subscription
nodes.summary: Cancels an existing subscription for a user/product pair, raising 404 if not found.

nodes.name: get_subscriptions
nodes.summary: Lists all active subscriptions for a user.

nodes.name: get_all_subscriptions_cart
nodes.summary: Builds a cart containing all of a user's subscribed products, matching each against the catalog and caching the resulting cart.

nodes.name: get_due_subscriptions
nodes.summary: Returns subscriptions currently due for reorder along with a pre-built cart of those items.

nodes.name: get_predicted_cart
nodes.summary: Returns a predicted restock cart for a user based on order-history pattern analysis.

nodes.name: get_user_preferences
nodes.summary: Returns a computed preference profile (brand affinity, category frequency, etc.) for a user.

nodes.name: get_recently_ordered
nodes.summary: Builds a list of recently-ordered and pantry-staple products for a user, falling back to random catalog picks when history is sparse.

nodes.name: replan_cart
nodes.summary: Re-plans a user's cart based on free-text feedback using the outcome service and returns the updated cart.

nodes.name: get_counterfactuals
nodes.summary: Returns explanations for why candidate products were rejected in favor of the selected cart item.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the domain model classes (Cart, CartItem, Substitution, IntentMode, NeedStatus, Need, Order, Product, User, UserLocation) for convenient importing.

nodes.name: order.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Order domain model representing a placed order with items, total, status, and payment details.

nodes.name: Order
nodes.summary: Domain model for a persisted order: id, user, date, items, total, status, and payment method/status.

nodes.name: product.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Product domain model representing a catalog item with pricing, stock, rating, and tag metadata.

nodes.name: Product
nodes.summary: Domain model for a catalog product including pricing, brand, category, stock, delivery ETA, and a price-per-unit helper property.

nodes.name: user.py
nodes.summary: Defines the User and UserLocation domain models representing account and location/demographic data.

nodes.name: UserLocation
nodes.summary: Domain model holding a user's city, state, and region.

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: Domain model for a user account including preferences, dietary tags, price tier, location, age, and gender.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Repository/cache factory module providing get_repository() and get_cache() singletons, selecting DynamoDB or in-memory repository based on configuration.

nodes.name: get_repository
nodes.summary: Returns a memoized Repository instance, choosing DynamoDBRepository or MemoryRepository based on settings.

nodes.name: get_cache
nodes.summary: Returns a memoized CacheLayer singleton instance.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract Repository interface with methods for product and user CRUD and order storage, implemented by concrete repositories.

nodes.name: Repository
nodes.summary: Abstract base class declaring the repository contract: get/list/upsert product, get/upsert user, get/upsert orders.

nodes.name: cache.py
nodes.summary: Cache abstraction layer with an in-process fallback and an optional Redis-backed store, used to cache carts, stock overrides, and generic responses.

nodes.name: _MemoryCache
nodes.summary: Simple in-process TTL cache used as a fallback when Redis is unavailable, backed by dictionaries for values and expirations.

nodes.name: CacheLayer
nodes.summary: Facade over Redis (with in-memory fallback) exposing typed helpers for cart storage, stock overrides, and generic cached-response get/set operations.

nodes.name: memory.py
nodes.summary: In-memory implementation of the Repository interface storing products, users, and orders in plain dictionaries; used for local development/testing.

nodes.name: MemoryRepository
nodes.summary: Dictionary-backed Repository implementation providing product search/list, user get/upsert, and order get/upsert without external storage.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Services package barrel that lazily proxies attribute access to catalog_service to preserve backward-compatible imports.

nodes.name: __getattr__
nodes.summary: Module-level __getattr__ hook that lazily forwards unresolved attribute lookups to the catalog_service submodule, raising AttributeError otherwise.

nodes.name: badge_service.py
nodes.summary: Computes and persists product 'badges' (e.g. bestseller, trending) from order history and stores per-item ratings submitted by users.

nodes.name: BadgeService
nodes.summary: Service that recomputes product badges from aggregated order counts per user/category and records individual item ratings against products.

nodes.name: get_badge_service
nodes.summary: Returns a memoized BadgeService singleton instance.

nodes.name: cart_ops_service.py
nodes.summary: Core cart-mutation service providing get/save/add/remove/update/clear operations on a user's cached cart, including economical-alternative substitution logic.

nodes.name: _find_economical_alternative
nodes.summary: Searches the catalog for a cheaper in-stock alternative to a requested item and builds a substitute CartItem if one is found.

nodes.name: CartOpsService
nodes.summary: Service encapsulating cart CRUD operations (get, save, add item, remove item, update quantity, get total, clear) backed by the cache layer and catalog service.

nodes.name: get_cart_ops_service
nodes.summary: Returns a memoized CartOpsService singleton instance.

nodes.name: catalog_service.py
nodes.summary: Central catalog service implementing product search, relevance scoring, recommendations, availability checks, and fuzzy need-to-product matching used throughout the intent-capture pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a hand-rolled relevance-scoring algorithm combining token overlap, brand/category matches, and regex-based unit stripping rather than an external search engine.

nodes.name: _compute_relevance_score
nodes.summary: Computes a heuristic relevance score between a search query and a product using token overlap, brand/category boosts, and normalized product-name matching.

nodes.name: CatalogService
nodes.summary: Service providing product retrieval, search, category browsing, recommendations, stock/availability checks, and fuzzy need-matching against the product catalog, backed by the repository and cache.

nodes.name: get_catalog_service
nodes.summary: Returns a memoized CatalogService singleton instance.

nodes.name: counterfactual_service.py
nodes.summary: Generates human-readable explanations for why alternative products were rejected in favor of a selected cart item, based on price, rating, and preference signals.

nodes.name: RejectedAlternative
nodes.summary: Value object describing a rejected candidate product with its price, score, rejection reasons, and a to_dict serializer.

nodes.name: CounterfactualService
nodes.summary: Service that compares a selected product against rejected candidates and generates ranked rejection reasons (price, rating, brand affinity) for cart-item explanations.

nodes.name: get_counterfactual_service
nodes.summary: Returns a memoized CounterfactualService singleton instance.

nodes.name: pantry_service.py
nodes.summary: Derives a user's recently-ordered/pantry-staple products from order history with days-since-last-order metadata.

nodes.name: PantryService
nodes.summary: Service that aggregates a user's order history into a deduplicated, recency-sorted list of previously purchased items.

nodes.name: get_pantry_service
nodes.summary: Returns a memoized PantryService singleton instance.

nodes.name: preference_service.py
nodes.summary: Computes and caches a user's shopping-preference profile (brand affinity, category frequency, price tier, product loyalty) from order history for personalization.

nodes.name: UserPreference
nodes.summary: Data model representing a computed user preference profile with brand affinity, category frequency, price tier, and top products/brands.

nodes.name: PreferenceService
nodes.summary: Service that computes, caches, and applies UserPreference profiles, including a preference-boost scorer and personalized reasoning text generator.

nodes.name: get_preference_service
nodes.summary: Returns a memoized PreferenceService singleton instance.

nodes.name: subscribe_service.py
nodes.summary: Largest service in the batch: predicts restock needs from order-history patterns, builds starter carts for new users, and manages recurring product subscriptions (add/remove/due/build-cart).

nodes.name: PredictedNeed
nodes.summary: Value object representing a predicted restock need for a product, including confidence and days-until-needed.

nodes.name: SubscribeService
nodes.summary: Service implementing restock prediction from purchase-interval analysis, new-user starter carts, subscription CRUD, and due-cart building, persisting subscriptions via the cache layer.

nodes.name: get_subscribe_service
nodes.summary: Returns a memoized SubscribeService singleton instance.

nodes.name: get_predictive_service
nodes.summary: Alias factory that returns the same singleton as get_subscribe_service for backward-compatible naming.

nodes.name: sqs_publisher.py
nodes.summary: Wraps AWS SQS to publish asynchronous job messages (e.g. background tasks) with job type/status enums and a singleton publisher accessor.

nodes.name: JobType
nodes.summary: Enum of supported async job types processed via SQS.

nodes.name: JobStatus
nodes.summary: Enum of possible statuses for an async job's lifecycle.

nodes.name: SQSPublisher
nodes.summary: Publishes job messages to an AWS SQS queue, lazily creating a boto3 client and serializing payloads for downstream workers.

nodes.name: get_sqs_publisher
nodes.summary: Factory function returning a singleton SQSPublisher instance.

nodes.name: meta.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI controller exposing operational/meta endpoints: system stats, system info, AWS cost breakdown, and an observability dashboard for monitoring the deployed service.

nodes.name: system_stats
nodes.summary: Endpoint handler returning a live metrics snapshot of the running application.

nodes.name: system_info
nodes.summary: Endpoint handler returning system/environment info such as configuration and runtime details.

nodes.name: _fetch_aws_costs
nodes.summary: Queries AWS Cost Explorer via boto3 to compute service-level cost breakdown for a recent date range.

nodes.name: cost_breakdown
nodes.summary: Endpoint handler exposing AWS cost breakdown data fetched via _fetch_aws_costs.

nodes.name: observability_dashboard
nodes.summary: Endpoint handler serving an observability dashboard view combining metrics and cost data.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Central application settings (Pydantic Settings) covering environment, LLM provider selection and API keys, data backend, caching, and semantic search/prediction tuning, exposed via a cached get_settings() accessor.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Pydantic settings model defining all configurable application parameters, including LLM provider keys and feature flags, with helper properties for parsing comma-separated lists.

nodes.name: get_settings
nodes.summary: Cached accessor returning the singleton Settings instance for use across the app.

nodes.name: logging.py
nodes.summary: Configures the application's root logging handler/formatter and provides a get_logger helper for module-scoped loggers.

nodes.name: setup_logging
nodes.summary: Initializes root logger with a stream handler and standard formatter at the given level.

nodes.name: get_logger
nodes.summary: Returns a named logger instance, ensuring setup_logging has run first.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the llm module, re-exporting the base provider interfaces and factory functions.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines abstract base interfaces LLMProvider and VisionProvider that concrete LLM/vision providers (Groq, Gemini, Bedrock, Mock) implement.

nodes.name: LLMProvider
nodes.summary: Abstract interface for text LLM providers, declaring complete_json and complete_text methods.

nodes.name: VisionProvider
nodes.summary: Abstract interface for vision-capable LLM providers, declaring a describe_image method.

nodes.name: bedrock_provider.py
nodes.summary: LLMProvider implementation backed by AWS Bedrock's converse API, with response caching and JSON/text completion methods.

nodes.name: _cache_key
nodes.summary: Computes a deterministic SHA-256 cache key from system/user prompt content.

nodes.name: BedrockProvider
nodes.summary: LLMProvider implementation using AWS Bedrock's converse API with cache-backed JSON and text completion.

nodes.name: factory.py
nodes.summary: Factory selecting the active text and vision LLM providers (Groq, Bedrock, Gemini, or Mock) based on application settings.

nodes.name: get_text_provider
nodes.summary: Selects and instantiates a text LLM provider (Groq, Bedrock, Gemini, or Mock fallback) based on configured API keys.

nodes.name: get_vision_provider
nodes.summary: Selects and instantiates a vision-capable LLM provider (Gemini vision or Mock fallback) based on configured API keys.

nodes.name: gemini_provider.py
nodes.summary: LLMProvider and VisionProvider implementations backed by Google Gemini, supporting API key rotation and JSON/text/image completion with fenced-code stripping.

nodes.name: _create_model
nodes.summary: Configures the Gemini SDK with an API key and returns a GenerativeModel instance.

nodes.name: _strip_fences
nodes.summary: Strips markdown code fences from raw LLM text output prior to JSON parsing.

nodes.name: GeminiProvider
nodes.summary: LLMProvider implementation using Google Gemini text models with API key cycling for rate-limit resilience.

nodes.name: GeminiVisionProvider
nodes.summary: VisionProvider implementation using Google Gemini's multimodal models to describe images with API key cycling.

nodes.name: groq_provider.py
nodes.summary: LLMProvider implementation backed by the Groq API, with API key/client rotation, response caching, and JSON/text completion.

nodes.name: _cache_key
nodes.summary: Computes a deterministic SHA-256 cache key from system/user prompt content.

nodes.name: GroqProvider
nodes.summary: LLMProvider implementation using the Groq chat completions API with client cycling across API keys and cache-backed responses.

nodes.name: mock_provider.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic mock LLM/vision providers used as fallbacks or for local development/testing when no real LLM API key is configured.

nodes.name: MockProvider
nodes.summary: Mock LLMProvider returning canned responses from a goal/recipe book for offline development and testing.

nodes.name: MockVisionProvider
nodes.summary: Mock VisionProvider returning a canned image description for offline development and testing.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: Application entry point wiring up the FastAPI app: lifespan startup seeding data/catalog/semantic index, middleware registration, router inclusion, and exception handlers.

nodes.name: lifespan
nodes.summary: FastAPI lifespan context manager that seeds repository data, builds the product catalog, mock users/orders, and the semantic search index on startup.

nodes.name: create_app
nodes.summary: Constructs and configures the FastAPI application instance, registering middleware, routers, and exception handlers.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the PII redaction, rate limiting, request ID, and telemetry middleware classes.

nodes.name: pii_redaction.py
nodes.summary: Middleware and regex-based helper that redacts emails and phone numbers from request bodies before logging for privacy compliance.

nodes.name: redact_pii
nodes.summary: Applies email and phone number regex substitutions to redact PII from a text string.

nodes.name: PiiRedactionMiddleware
nodes.summary: Starlette middleware that redacts PII from incoming request bodies before they are logged.

nodes.name: rate_limit.py
nodes.summary: Token-bucket based rate limiting middleware that throttles requests per client, protecting the API from abuse.

nodes.name: _TokenBucket
nodes.summary: Token bucket algorithm implementation tracking consumption and refill rate for rate limiting.

nodes.name: RateLimitMiddleware
nodes.summary: Starlette middleware enforcing per-client rate limits using per-client token buckets, returning 429 responses when exceeded.

nodes.name: request_id.py
nodes.summary: Middleware that assigns or propagates a request correlation ID for tracing requests across logs.

nodes.name: RequestIdMiddleware
nodes.summary: Starlette middleware that generates or forwards a unique request ID header for each request.

nodes.name: telemetry.py
nodes.summary: Middleware that records per-request latency, error, and cart metrics into an in-process metrics store, exposing get_metrics_snapshot for the meta/observability endpoints.

nodes.name: get_metrics_snapshot
nodes.summary: Computes and returns a snapshot dict of aggregated request metrics (latency percentiles, error rates, cache hit rate, per-path counts) for observability endpoints.

nodes.name: RequestMetrics
nodes.summary: Data container holding aggregate request/latency/cache/LLM metrics accumulated across the app's lifetime.

nodes.name: TelemetryMiddleware
nodes.summary: Starlette middleware that times each request and updates the shared RequestMetrics store with latency, status, and cart-related statistics.

nodes.name: confidence_service.py
nodes.summary: Scores confidence of matched cart items and flags low-confidence items or ambiguous matches that need user clarification.

nodes.name: ConfidenceService
nodes.summary: Computes overall and per-item confidence scores for a cart and determines whether clarification is needed from the user.

nodes.name: get_confidence_service
nodes.summary: Factory function returning a singleton ConfidenceService instance.

nodes.name: semantic_search_service.py
nodes.summary: Hybrid product retrieval service combining bi-encoder embedding search, cross-encoder reranking, and RapidFuzz fallback to match user query text against the product catalog, with embedding caching to disk.

nodes.name: _expand_with_synonyms
nodes.summary: Expands a search query with known synonyms to improve match recall.

nodes.name: _build_product_text
nodes.summary: Builds a synonym-expanded text representation of a product for embedding/indexing.

nodes.name: _cache_key
nodes.summary: Computes a deterministic cache key from a query and candidate product IDs.

nodes.name: get_semantic_search_service
nodes.summary: Factory function returning a singleton HybridRetrievalService instance.

nodes.name: HybridRetrievalService
nodes.summary: Core retrieval engine that loads bi-encoder/cross-encoder models, builds and caches product embeddings, and performs hybrid semantic + fuzzy search with reranking for product matching.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SentenceTransformer bi-encoder for candidate retrieval and a cross-encoder for reranking, with disk-cached embeddings and rapidfuzz as a lexical fallback.

nodes.name: dynamodb.py
nodes.summary: DynamoDB repository implementing the primary datastore access layer: table provisioning and CRUD operations for products, users, and orders via aioboto3, with helpers to convert between Python floats and DynamoDB Decimal types.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses aioboto3 async DynamoDB resource sessions and recursive helpers to convert between float and Decimal for DynamoDB compatibility.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the seed module that re-exports catalog loading, mock data generation, and stock override helpers as the public seed API.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for `python -m app.seed`; orchestrates loading the catalog, applying stock overrides, seeding mock users/orders, and writing everything into the configured repository and cache.

nodes.name: catalog.py
nodes.summary: Loads and normalizes the product catalog from CSV or XLSX datasets into domain Product models, generating stable product IDs, parsing units, tags, ratings, and descriptions.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports both CSV (stdlib csv) and XLSX (openpyxl) dataset formats with a shared normalization pipeline.

nodes.name: mock_data.py
nodes.summary: Generates hardcoded mock User and Order domain objects used to seed the demo/dev environment with realistic sample data.
nodes.languageNotes: create_mock_orders is a very large function (275 lines) composed almost entirely of repeated literal Order construction blocks.

nodes.name: seed_dynamodb.py
nodes.summary: High-level seeding routine that provisions DynamoDB tables and bulk-loads catalog, stock override, mock user, and mock order data into DynamoDB, plus a helper to check whether seeding already occurred.

nodes.name: stock_overrides.py
nodes.summary: Derives a deterministic subset of product IDs to be marked as out-of-stock/overridden for demo purposes, based on a hash of the full product ID set.

nodes.name: _session_kwargs
nodes.summary: Builds the keyword arguments (endpoint, region, credentials) used to construct the aioboto3 DynamoDB resource session, called by nearly every repository method.

nodes.name: DynamoDBRepository
nodes.summary: Primary datastore repository providing async table creation and CRUD operations (get/list/upsert) for products, users, and orders against DynamoDB.

nodes.name: _run_seed
nodes.summary: Async driver that loads the catalog, computes stock overrides, bulk-upserts products, seeds mock users and orders into the repository/cache, and prints a summary report with timing.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Synchronous CLI entry point that runs `_run_seed` via asyncio and exits with a non-zero status on failure.

nodes.name: _safe_float
nodes.summary: Safely coerces a raw dataset cell value to a float, falling back to a default when the value is blank or unparsable.

nodes.name: _parse_unit_from_name
nodes.summary: Extracts a quantity and unit (e.g. '500 ml') from a product name string using a regular expression.

nodes.name: load_catalog
nodes.summary: Reads the product dataset (CSV or XLSX), normalizes each row into a domain Product with generated IDs, tags, units, ratings, and descriptions, and returns the full product list.

nodes.name: _read_xlsx
nodes.summary: Reads rows from an XLSX dataset file into a list of row values using openpyxl.

nodes.name: create_mock_users
nodes.summary: Builds a fixed list of sample User domain objects used for local development and demo seeding.

nodes.name: create_mock_orders
nodes.summary: Generates a large fixed list of sample Order domain objects referencing given product IDs, covering varied order states and timelines for demo seeding.

nodes.name: seed_dynamodb
nodes.summary: Ensures DynamoDB tables exist and, if not already populated (or forced), loads the catalog and mock data and bulk-writes products, users, and orders into DynamoDB.

nodes.name: is_dynamodb_seeded
nodes.summary: Checks whether DynamoDB already contains seeded product data by creating tables if needed and querying the product list.

nodes.name: get_override_product_ids
nodes.summary: Deterministically selects a subset of the given product IDs to treat as stock overrides, ensuring consistent demo behavior across runs.

nodes.name: AddressManager.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal/sheet UI component for managing saved delivery addresses, letting users detect their location via GPS reverse-geocoding or add/edit addresses manually with labels (Home/Work/Other).
nodes.languageNotes: Single large functional component with many useState hooks driving a multi-step sheet UI (list -> GPS detect -> manual entry).

nodes.name: AddressManager
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component rendering the address management sheet: lists saved addresses, handles GPS detection, manual address entry, activation, and deletion.

nodes.name: Header.tsx
nodes.summary: Site header component with search bar (animated placeholder), navigation category links, cart button, user menu, PWA install trigger, and the delivery address selector that opens AddressManager.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains a custom useAnimatedPlaceholder hook defined and used locally within the same file for a typewriter-style search placeholder effect.

nodes.name: useAnimatedPlaceholder
nodes.summary: Local hook that cycles through placeholder search phrases with a typewriter-style typing/erasing animation using timed setTimeout callbacks.

nodes.name: Header
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component rendering the app header: search, category navigation, cart summary, user menu, address selector, and iOS PWA install instructions.

nodes.name: PwaInstallPrompt.tsx
nodes.summary: Dismissible toast/banner component that prompts eligible users to install the PWA after a delay, persisting dismissal state to localStorage.

nodes.name: PwaInstallPrompt
nodes.summary: Default-exported component that shows a delayed install prompt using usePwaInstall, and lets the user install or dismiss (with localStorage-backed persistence).

nodes.name: LocationContext.tsx
nodes.summary: React context that wraps the useDeliveryLocation hook so delivery-location state is shared app-wide via a single provider instance, exposing a useLocation() accessor hook.

nodes.name: LocationProvider
nodes.summary: Provider component that instantiates useDeliveryLocation once and exposes its value to descendants via LocationContext.

nodes.name: useLocation
nodes.summary: Consumer hook that reads the LocationContext value, throwing if used outside a LocationProvider; the recommended way for components to access delivery-location state.

nodes.name: useLocation.ts
nodes.summary: Core delivery-location logic: localStorage-backed saved addresses, Nominatim reverse-geocoding for GPS coordinates, and the useDeliveryLocation hook exposing address CRUD, active-address selection, and GPS detection.
nodes.languageNotes: reverseGeocode calls the OpenStreetMap Nominatim API and prioritizes address components (quarter, industrial, residential, road) to build a human-readable block/area string.

nodes.name: loadAddresses
nodes.summary: Reads and JSON-parses the saved addresses list from localStorage, returning an empty array on failure.

nodes.name: saveAddresses
nodes.summary: Serializes and writes the addresses list to localStorage.

nodes.name: reverseGeocode
nodes.summary: Reverse-geocodes GPS latitude/longitude via the Nominatim API and derives a readable block/area string from the returned address components.

nodes.name: useDeliveryLocation
nodes.summary: Exported hook managing delivery-location state: saved addresses, active address id, GPS-based detection with fallback accuracy handling, add/remove/update address, all persisted to localStorage.

nodes.name: usePwaInstall.ts
nodes.summary: Hook that detects PWA install eligibility across platforms (standalone mode detection, iOS Safari heuristics, beforeinstallprompt capture) and exposes a triggerInstall function.

nodes.name: isRunningAsInstalledPwa
nodes.summary: Detects whether the app is currently running in standalone/installed PWA mode via matchMedia checks.

nodes.name: usePwaInstall
nodes.summary: Exported hook tracking PWA install state (installed, installable, iOS-safari) by listening for the beforeinstallprompt event and exposing a triggerInstall action.

nodes.name: Button.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared button UI primitive with variant (primary/secondary/accent/outline/ghost) and size styling, a built-in loading spinner state, and left/right icon slots used across the client app.

nodes.name: Button
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component that renders a styled <button> combining variant/size class maps, disables itself while loading, and swaps in a Spinner when the loading prop is true.

nodes.name: Spinner.tsx
nodes.summary: Small reusable SVG loading spinner component with configurable size, className, and accessible label, used inside Button and other async UI states.

nodes.name: Spinner
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component rendering an animated spinner icon sized and styled via props, with an accessible loading label.

nodes.name: States.tsx
nodes.summary: UI primitives for common empty and error states, each accepting an icon/title/description and an optional retry/action Button, used to give consistent feedback screens across the app.

nodes.name: EmptyState
nodes.summary: Named-exported component that renders a centered empty-state layout with icon, title, description, and an optional action element.

nodes.name: ErrorState
nodes.summary: Named-exported component that renders an error message with a default title/description and an optional retry Button wired to an onRetry callback.

nodes.name: Panel.tsx
nodes.summary: Accessible modal/bottom-sheet UI primitive used by the app's front doors — traps focus, locks body scroll, and closes on Escape or backdrop click, rendering as a bottom sheet on mobile and centered dialog on larger screens.
nodes.languageNotes: Manages iOS Safari scroll-lock quirks by fixing body position and restoring scrollY on close.

nodes.name: Panel
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component rendering an accessible dialog/bottom-sheet with header (icon, title, subtitle, close button), scrollable body, and optional footer; handles focus trapping and Escape-to-close.

nodes.name: Toast.tsx
nodes.summary: Toast notification system providing a React context provider and hook for showing dismissible, auto-expiring toast messages across the app.

nodes.name: ToastItem
nodes.summary: Renders a single toast notification and auto-dismisses it after a timeout, clearing the timer on unmount.

nodes.name: ToastProvider
nodes.summary: Context provider component that maintains toast state and exposes add/dismiss callbacks to descendant components, rendering the active toast list.

nodes.name: useToast
nodes.summary: Custom hook that reads the toast context, giving consumers access to the toast list and dispatch functions.

nodes.name: Transition.tsx
nodes.summary: Lightweight animation wrapper components (fade and pop-in entrance transitions) reused by other UI primitives like Panel.

nodes.name: FadeIn
nodes.summary: Wraps children in a div that applies a fade-in entrance animation, supporting an optional delay and pass-through className/style/props.

nodes.name: PopIn
nodes.summary: Wraps children in a div that applies a pop/scale-in entrance animation, supporting an optional delay and pass-through className/style/props; used by Panel for dialog entrance.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for the NowCart Vite/React/TypeScript frontend: compiles static assets with Node in a builder stage, then serves them via a hardened Nginx runtime image with SPA routing/API proxy config and a health check.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-stage build (builder -> runtime) keeps the final image slim by discarding Node/npm and source files, copying only the compiled /app/dist output into the nginx image.

nodes.name: builder
nodes.summary: Build stage based on node:20-alpine that installs npm dependencies with `npm ci` and runs `npm run build` to produce the Vite production bundle.

nodes.name: runtime
nodes.summary: Runtime stage based on nginx:alpine that replaces the default nginx config with a custom SPA-routing/API-proxy config, copies the built assets from the builder stage, exposes port 80, and defines a container health check.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for the FastAPI backend: a builder stage installs Python dependencies via uv into a virtualenv, and a slim runtime stage copies the venv plus application code, runs as a non-root user, exposes port 8000, and starts uvicorn with a health check for EC2 deployment.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-stage build separates the uv-based dependency install (builder) from the runtime image, keeping the final image slim; runs as an unprivileged 'nowcart' user and defines a HEALTHCHECK for orchestration/monitoring.

nodes.name: builder
nodes.summary: Build stage based on python:3.11-slim that installs the uv package manager and resolves/installs Python dependencies from server/pyproject.toml and server/uv.lock into an isolated virtualenv at /app/.venv.

nodes.name: runtime
nodes.summary: Final slim python:3.11-slim runtime image that copies the prebuilt virtualenv and application code, adds the BigBasket.csv data file, drops privileges to a non-root user, exposes port 8000, defines a HEALTHCHECK, and launches the app with uvicorn.

nodes.name: deploy.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow that deploys NowCart on every push to master: builds the Vite frontend and syncs it to S3 with CloudFront cache invalidation, then SSHes into the EC2 host to write the production .env, git pull the backend, and restart the systemd nowcart service.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-job GitHub Actions pipeline with a `needs` dependency ensuring the backend job only runs after the frontend deploy succeeds; secrets are injected via appleboy/ssh-action to write both an app-local .env and a systemd EnvironmentFile.

nodes.name: deploy-frontend
nodes.summary: Job that checks out the code, installs and builds the client with npm, then syncs the built dist/ folder to the nowcart-frontend-strizzy S3 bucket and invalidates the CloudFront distribution cache.

nodes.name: deploy-backend
nodes.summary: Job that runs after deploy-frontend succeeds; SSHes into the production EC2 instance to write environment secrets to /opt/nowcart/server/.env and /etc/nowcart.env, then git pulls the latest master branch and restarts the nowcart systemd service.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Excludes Python virtualenv/cache dirs, Node modules/build output, IDE/OS artifacts, and .env secret files from Docker build contexts.

nodes.name: APK_BUILD_GUIDE.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step guide for building, signing, and hosting the NowCart Android APK (Android Studio, Gradle signing config, S3/EC2 hosting) plus notes on the already-live PWA.

nodes.name: ARCHITECTURE_DIAGRAM.md
nodes.summary: High-level visual architecture diagram of NowCart with a one-minute walkthrough of how requests flow through the system.

nodes.name: NowCart_Presentation_Script.md
nodes.summary: Full slide-by-slide presentation script for a hackathon grand finale pitch, covering the product story, demo voiceover, technical architecture, algorithms, and growth roadmap.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project README describing NowCart's purpose, how it works, its architecture, tech stack, local development setup, deployment, and admin access.

nodes.name: SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive system architecture document detailing the LangGraph Outcome Engine node-by-node (intent, decompose, pantry filter, match/RAG, optimize, substitute, confidence, counterfactual), the Zero Door predictive restock path, data flow diagrams for each entry mode, middleware stack, LLM provider architecture, hybrid retrieval, infrastructure, scaling, observability, and security hardening.

nodes.name: archi.md
nodes.summary: Hybrid system architecture diagram and one-minute presentation script explaining key implementation details behind the diagram.

nodes.name: deploy.bat
nodes.summary: Windows batch script that builds the frontend, syncs the build to an S3 bucket, invalidates the CloudFront distribution, and reminds the operator to SSH into EC2 to restart the backend service.
nodes.languageNotes: Sequential 4-step batch script with %errorlevel% checks after each AWS CLI call; hardcodes a local dev path and specific S3 bucket/CloudFront distribution IDs.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Local development Docker Compose stack wiring together the FastAPI backend, nginx-served Vite frontend, Redis (cache/session), and DynamoDB Local (persistence), each with health checks and a shared bridge network.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses depends_on with service_healthy conditions to sequence startup: dynamodb-local/redis before backend, backend before frontend.

nodes.name: backend
nodes.summary: FastAPI backend service built from server/Dockerfile, configured via environment variables for DynamoDB, Redis, Groq/Gemini LLM providers, and CORS; exposes port 8000.

nodes.name: frontend
nodes.summary: Nginx-served Vite frontend built from client/Dockerfile, exposed on port 3000 and depending on the backend being healthy.

nodes.name: redis
nodes.summary: Redis 7 Alpine service providing cart/session state and LLM response caching, with a persistent volume and periodic save policy.

nodes.name: dynamodb-local
nodes.summary: Local DynamoDB emulator service (amazon/dynamodb-local) for product/user/order persistence during development, backed by a persistent volume.

nodes.name: requirements.md
nodes.summary: Kiro spec-driven requirements document for NowCart defining 10 numbered requirements (Outcome Engine, Voice-to-Order, Constraint-First Ordering, Show It/Share It, Comparison Collapse & Confidence Score, Substitution Intelligence, SOS/Emergency Mode, Catalog & Data, Architecture/Scaling/Cost, and Frontend Revamp & Backend Sync) each with acceptance criteria, plus an introduction and glossary.

nodes.name: design.md
nodes.summary: Architecture and design document for NowCart describing the layered backend (controllers/middleware/services/agents/repositories/llm/models), the LangGraph Outcome Engine node pipeline, REST API surface, data models (Product/Cart/Need), storage layout (DynamoDB/Redis), error handling, privacy/scaling strategy, and seven correctness properties that the implementation must satisfy.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents an intent-capture engine where multiple input front doors (voice/text/budget/photo/share-link) normalize into a shared AgentState and flow through a LangGraph multi-agent pipeline (intent -> decompose -> match -> optimize -> substitute -> confidence -> HITL).

nodes.name: tasks.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for NowCart breaking work into 9 dependency-ordered waves (foundation/LLM providers, data layer + seed, catalog matching, LangGraph Outcome Engine, supporting services, REST API + middleware, React/Vite/TS frontend, frontend revamp & backend sync, verify + deploy), with per-task verification steps and completion checkboxes.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Boilerplate Vite React+TypeScript README describing the default template, React Compiler note, and guidance for expanding the ESLint configuration.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite entry HTML for the NowCart PWA client, wiring PWA meta tags, Apple touch icons, Google Fonts, and mounting the React app via src/main.tsx.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes PWA-specific meta tags (mobile-web-app-capable, apple-mobile-web-app-*, theme-color) indicating the client is installable as a Progressive Web App.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: NPM manifest for the NowCart React client, defining Vite build/dev scripts, OpenAPI type generation, Capacitor Android packaging, and dependencies including React 19, Tailwind CSS v4, react-router-dom, and lucide-react.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes a gen:types script running openapi-typescript against a local FastAPI backend's /openapi.json, and a build:apk script that syncs the web build into a Capacitor Android project.

nodes.name: tsconfig.app.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the client application source, setting bundler module resolution and strict type-checking options for files under src/.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Root TypeScript project reference file that composes tsconfig.app.json and tsconfig.node.json for the client's split app/tooling build configuration.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for Node-context tooling files (e.g. vite.config.ts), enabling strict type-checking with bundler module resolution separate from the app build.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code project settings file listing enabled plugins for the local development assistant.

nodes.name: .config.kiro
nodes.summary: Marker/config file for the Kiro spec-driven development tool tied to the user-authentication feature spec.

nodes.name: requirements.md
nodes.summary: Requirements document for a user-authentication feature (signup, OTP verification, login, JWT token management, auth UI, session persistence/logout, and user data storage) written in EARS acceptance-criteria format.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern list controlling which files and directories the Understand-Anything knowledge graph scanner excludes from analysis.

nodes.name: capacitor.config.ts
nodes.summary: Capacitor configuration for packaging the NowCart web client as a native mobile app, defining app ID, name, and web directory settings.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: ESLint flat-config for the client codebase, defining linting rules and plugin setup for TypeScript/React source files.

nodes.name: gen-icons.mjs
nodes.summary: Node build script that generates app icon assets (favicons/mobile icons) for the client from a source image.

nodes.name: nginx.conf
nodes.summary: Nginx server configuration used to serve the built client SPA and proxy API requests in production/container deployment.

nodes.name: schema.d.ts
nodes.summary: Auto-generated TypeScript type definitions describing the backend API's request/response schema, used for typed API client calls.
nodes.languageNotes: Large generated declaration file (868 lines), likely produced by an OpenAPI-to-TypeScript generator.

nodes.name: Button.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable styled Button UI component supporting variants, sizes, loading state, and icon slots.

nodes.name: Button
nodes.summary: React functional component rendering a button element with variant/size/loading/icon props, composing class names via the cn utility.

nodes.name: cn.ts
nodes.summary: Tiny utility module exporting a class-name concatenation helper used across UI components.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Joins truthy class name fragments into a single space-separated string, similar to the classnames/clsx pattern.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet defining base styles, CSS custom properties (design tokens like shadows/colors), and Tailwind layer imports for the client app.

nodes.name: SosPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Minimal placeholder page component for an SOS/emergency-help route in the client app.

nodes.name: SosPage
nodes.summary: Trivial React page component stub for the SOS page route.

nodes.name: Card.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable Card UI component providing surface styling, padding variants, and optional interactive hover states.

nodes.name: Card
nodes.summary: React functional component rendering a styled card container with configurable padding and interactive hover/shadow behavior.

nodes.name: Chip.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable Chip/badge UI component with tone and size variants, optionally rendering a leading icon.

nodes.name: Chip
nodes.summary: React functional component rendering a pill-shaped chip with tone-based coloring and optional icon.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration for the client app, defining plugins, dev server settings, and build output options.

nodes.name: add_5000_more.py
nodes.summary: Data-generation utility script that appends roughly 5000 additional product/catalog records, verifying associated image URLs before inclusion.

nodes.name: verify_image
nodes.summary: Validates that a catalog row's image URL is reachable and returns a real image by issuing an HTTP request and checking content length/headers.

nodes.name: check_cors.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic script for verifying CORS headers/behavior of the NowCart backend API.

nodes.name: check_page2.py
nodes.summary: Small ad-hoc script for inspecting/testing a second page of paginated data or results, likely used during catalog debugging.

nodes.name: create_verified_csv.py
nodes.summary: Data-pipeline script that builds a CSV of catalog records after verifying each entry's image URL is valid and reachable.

nodes.name: check_image_url
nodes.summary: Checks whether a given image URL responds successfully via an HTTP HEAD/GET-style request before a row is written to the verified CSV.

nodes.name: find_placeholders.py
nodes.summary: Scans catalog data rows to detect placeholder or invalid product images by comparing downloaded content size against expected thresholds.

nodes.name: check_size
nodes.summary: Fetches a row's image URL and inspects the response content-length header to flag likely placeholder images.

nodes.name: fix_placeholders.py
nodes.summary: Remediation script that re-checks and replaces placeholder/broken product images identified in the catalog data.

nodes.name: check_real_image
nodes.summary: Verifies whether a catalog row's image URL points to a genuine (non-placeholder) image by inspecting HTTP response size and headers.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker file establishing the server.app Python package.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package docstring/init module describing the async_jobs architecture: API endpoint to SQS queue to Lambda function to DynamoDB result, with frontend polling for completion, used to offload heavy operations like vision analysis and cart decomposition.

nodes.name: lambda_handlers.py
nodes.summary: AWS Lambda handler stubs for offloaded async jobs — vision analysis, share/decomposition, and outcome processing — invoked via SQS to scale heavy operations outside the main API process.

nodes.name: vision_handler
nodes.summary: Lambda entry point that processes vision-analysis job events from SQS, parses payloads, runs analysis, and returns/logs results with error handling per item.

nodes.name: share_handler
nodes.summary: Lambda entry point handling share/cart-decomposition job events from SQS, parsing payloads and aggregating per-item results with error handling.

nodes.name: outcome_handler
nodes.summary: Lambda entry point processing outcome-tracking job events from SQS, parsing payloads and recording per-item success/failure results.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker file that makes server/app/core a Python package for core cross-cutting concerns (config, settings, shared infrastructure).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for server/app/models with a docstring documenting the two subpackages — `domain` for internal models (e.g. Cart, Product) and `dto` for wire contracts (e.g. CartResponse, OutcomeRequest) — instructing consumers to import directly from the subpackages rather than this barrel.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately does not re-export symbols; the docstring directs callers to import from app.models.domain and app.models.dto directly, so this is not a barrel despite being an __init__.py.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python project configuration for the nowcart-server package, declaring dependencies (FastAPI, LangGraph, LangChain, Groq, Google Generative AI, boto3/aioboto3, Redis, sentence-transformers, rapidfuzz, httpx, orjson, tenacity), dev/test dependencies, pytest asyncio settings, and the hatchling build backend.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses hatchling as the build backend and pytest-asyncio in auto mode, reflecting the async FastAPI + LangGraph architecture; combines web framework, LLM/agent, and AWS/data dependencies in one manifest.

layers.name: Frontend Application
layers.description: The React 19 + TypeScript client UI — pages, cart/frontdoor components, UI primitives, hooks, context, and the API client that talks to the FastAPI backend.

layers.name: Frontend Build & Tooling
layers.description: Vite, TypeScript, Capacitor, ESLint, and Nginx configuration that builds and serves the client PWA/Android app.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: FastAPI controllers and the app entrypoint that expose HTTP endpoints for cart, catalog, orders, vision, voice, and auth.

layers.name: Service & Agent Layer
layers.description: Business logic services plus the LangGraph agent graph/nodes and the LLM provider abstraction (Bedrock, Gemini, Groq, mock) that power intent capture.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Domain models, request/response DTOs, and repository implementations (DynamoDB, cache, in-memory) that persist and shape application data.

layers.name: Backend Cross-Cutting Support
layers.description: Middleware (PII redaction, rate limiting, telemetry), core config/logging, async job handlers, and database seeding utilities that support the backend runtime.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Deployment
layers.description: Dockerfiles, docker-compose services, the GitHub Actions deploy pipeline, and project-level tooling config that build, containerize, and ship the app.

layers.name: Documentation & Specs
layers.description: Project READMEs, architecture write-ups, and Kiro spec-driven-development requirement/design/task documents.

layers.name: Data Utility Scripts
layers.description: Standalone Python scripts for generating, verifying, and fixing catalog/product data outside the main application runtime.
